photo_captions: Münster zu Ulm S[?]. Höchste Kirche der Welt [?] Meter hoch.
pages.notes: Left page is a glued-in decorative printed postcard/lithograph of Ulm showing the Minster (Münster zu Ulm), labelled 'ULM an der Donau'. The postcard contains printed German text listing sights (Rathaus, Fischbrunnen, etc.) but is a printed item, not handwritten. No handwritten page number visible. The caption beneath the minster reads 'Münster zu Ulm S[?]. Höchste Kirche der Welt [?] Meter hoch.' — text is small and partially legible. A wheat/grain decorative motif appears at the bottom of the page.

pages.full_text:
it, but the children really enjoy it.
It's run by nice older women & the
children meet up with friends.
School is 8-12. They can stay at
the club 12-2 or 12-5.
After lunch we rested (v. hot) & then
went on a bike ride. Ferry - taking
bikes + 5 cars. Park. The children
played clock golf. Hot sun. R. [?Weckar]
Picnic. Chat - lovely. We saw village
Eric cycled home [?alone] [?hands]
the rest of us missed the ferry &
cycled over the bridge.
Wed [Thursday]. July 25th. The chil's school is over but
they have to go to church today,
which they don't want. Then holidays
until Sept 7th.
We left at 8.20. down motorway.
Traffic getting out of Ulm was awful
V. bad road signs. We enjoyed looking
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '12' visible at top right corner. Text begins mid-sentence, continuing from previous page. 'Wed' is written then crossed out/corrected to 'Thursday' (the word Thursday appears underlined or struck through). The river name reads '[?Weckar]' — likely 'Neckar' (R. Neckar) given the geographic context near Ulm; the initial letter is ambiguous in cursive. The word after 'alone' above 'hands' is difficult to read — may be a marginal annotation 'hands' added above the line. 'chil's' is an abbreviation for 'children's'. Year cannot be confirmed from page content alone; volume B covers 2003–2019 and page 12 suggests an early entry in the volume. The text cuts off at the bottom with 'We enjoyed looking', continuing on the next page. A decorative wheat/grain motif appears at the bottom centre of the page (same as left page, suggesting a consistent page border design).
